source_paper_title: The Information Projection in Moment Inequality Models: Existence, Dual Representation, and Approximation
context:
Section 6 (Random Interval Example), paragraph introducing SAA, p. 23

Naturally, the quality of the SAA solution (e.g., in terms of convergence rates) would depend on specifications of \(Q\) and \(\{f_\gamma : \gamma \in \Gamma\}\), and the size of the random sample, among other parameters. A formal analysis of convergence and statistical validation of using SAA within our approximation scheme goes beyond the intended scope of the paper, and we leave it for future research.
title: Convergence guarantees for Sample Average Approximation within the dual discretization scheme for infinite-dimensional I-projections
latex:
Given a probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,Q)\) and an integer \(n\ge 1\), let \(v_1,\dots,v_n\in L^1(Q)\) be fixed measurable functions. Define the convex objective
\[
F(\alpha,\mu) \;=\; \int_{\Omega} \exp\!\Big(\alpha\sum_{i=1}^n \mu_i v_i(\omega)\Big)\,dQ(\omega),
\]
for decision variables \(\alpha\in\mathbb R_+\) and \(\mu=(\mu_1,\dots,\mu_n)\in\mathbb R_+^n\) satisfying \(\sum_{i=1}^n \mu_i=1\). Assume \(F(\alpha,\mu)<\infty\) for all feasible \((\alpha,\mu)\).

Let \((\omega_j)_{j\ge 1}\) be i.i.d. samples from \(Q\). For each sample size \(N\ge 1\), define the Sample Average Approximation (SAA) objective
\[
F_N(\alpha,\mu) \;=\; \frac{1}{N}\sum_{j=1}^N \exp\!\Big(\alpha\sum_{i=1}^n \mu_i v_i(\omega_j)\Big).
\]
Consider the true problem and its SAA counterpart:
\[
\mathrm{OPT}\;=\;\inf\big\{F(\alpha,\mu): \alpha\ge 0,\ \mu\in\mathbb R_+^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n \mu_i=1\big\},
\]
\[
\mathrm{OPT}_N\;=\;\inf\big\{F_N(\alpha,\mu): \alpha\ge 0,\ \mu\in\mathbb R_+^n,\ \sum_{i=1}^n \mu_i=1\big\}.
\]
The problem is to provide verifiable conditions on \(Q\) and \(v_1,\dots,v_n\) under which, as \(N\to\infty\):
- \(\mathrm{OPT}_N\to \mathrm{OPT}\) (e.g., almost surely or in probability),
- every accumulation point of a sequence \((\alpha_N,\mu^{(N)})\in\arg\min F_N\) is an optimizer of the true problem,
- and to quantify finite-sample performance (e.g., nonasymptotic bounds or convergence rates for \(|\mathrm{OPT}_N-\mathrm{OPT}|\) and/or \(\|\mu^{(N)}-\mu^\star\|\)) in terms of \(N\) and distributional/complexity characteristics of \(\{v_i\}_{i=1}^n\).

A formal analysis of convergence and statistical validation of using SAA within our approximation scheme goes beyond the intended scope of the paper, and we leave it for future research.
